evals.expected_output: Position paper for Coder (coder.com) covering cloud development environments with buy/wait/no-buy recommendation
expectations: Output contains a clear BUY, WAIT, or NO BUY recommendation in the title/heading area
Output contains a 'What Coder Does' or equivalent section describing the product
Output contains a Company Snapshot section or table with funding, founding, and leadership details
Output contains a Benefits section with at least 4 numbered benefits
Output contains a Risks section presented as a table with severity and mitigation columns
Output contains a Competitive Moat Assessment section with at least 3 moat layers
Output contains a Feature Comparison table against at least one competitor (e.g., GitHub Codespaces)
Output contains a Market Context section with analyst or market trend references
Output contains a final Recommendation section with supporting rationale bullets
Output does NOT contain placeholder text like {Company} or {detail}

evals.expected_output: Position paper for Datadog with emphasis on cost/pricing analysis in the recommendation
expectations: Output contains a clear BUY, WAIT, or NO BUY recommendation
Output contains a product description section explaining what Datadog does
Output contains a Company Snapshot with financial details (Datadog is public, so revenue/growth should be available)
Output contains a Risks section that addresses pricing/cost concerns prominently
Output contains a Feature Comparison against at least one competitor in the observability space
Output contains a Recommendation section where cost considerations are weighted in the rationale
Output references Datadog's public financial data (revenue, growth rate, or market cap)

evals.expected_output: Position paper for Tailscale saved to docs/position-papers/tailscale-2026.md
expectations: Output contains a clear BUY, WAIT, or NO BUY recommendation
Output contains a product description covering Tailscale's mesh VPN / zero-trust networking
Output contains a Competitive Moat Assessment section
Output contains a Feature Comparison against at least one competitor (e.g., WireGuard, Cloudflare WARP, Zscaler)
A file was saved to docs/position-papers/tailscale-2026.md or similar path
The saved file contains the same structured position paper content

evals.prompt: /position Snowflake --context "3k-person healthcare SaaS, HIPAA + SOC2, multi-cloud (AWS + Azure), Okta SSO, must export audit logs to Splunk, data residency required in US and EU"
evals.expected_output: Position paper for Snowflake tailored to a regulated healthcare enterprise shape
expectations: Output contains a clear BUY, WAIT, or NO BUY recommendation
Output contains a product description of Snowflake's data cloud / warehouse
Output contains a Total Cost of Ownership section with license, implementation, operations, and training cost components
Output contains an Integration Checklist table covering SSO, SCIM, audit log export, API coverage, data residency, deployment options, and compliance certifications
Output contains an Exit & Data Portability section covering data export, API standards, migration path, and lock-in risk
Output explicitly references the stated context — HIPAA, SOC2, multi-cloud, Okta, Splunk, or US/EU data residency appear in the fit analysis
Output contains a 'Fit for your context' paragraph or equivalent near the Recommendation that ties the call back to the stated enterprise shape
Output does NOT contain placeholder text like {Company} or {detail}
